clinical_coherence.notes: 82M East Asian with HTN, DM2, moderate AS, CKD 3b, and BPH. Presenting after syncope while seated. HR 32 on presentation. Complete heart block with calcific AS and conduction system involvement. Three prior near-syncopal episodes over 6 weeks adds progressive conduction disease pattern. Age 82 is realistic for degenerative conduction disease.

tool_output_consistency.notes: ECG shows complete heart block with ventricular escape at 32 bpm. Holter confirms persistent CHB with pauses up to 4.6 seconds. EEG normal. Labs appropriate. All outputs concordant with calcific degenerative conduction disease.

ground_truth_quality.notes: R55 is acceptable. Diagnosis correctly identifies calcific AS with conduction system involvement. Critical actions include pacemaker planning and tamsulosin as contributing orthostatic agent.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Straightforward is appropriate. Complete heart block on ECG is immediately diagnostic.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 82M East Asian with extensive cardiovascular comorbidities is realistic. Age-appropriate for degenerative conduction disease. Adds ethnic diversity.

educational_value.notes: Clinically sound but the fourth straightforward case with complete heart block. Very similar to S01, S02, and S03 in diagnostic pattern. Limited incremental educational value. The East Asian demographic and advanced age are the main distinguishing features.

issues_found: Minor: R55 ICD code is symptom-level
Fourth straightforward case with essentially identical diagnosis (complete heart block), significantly reducing case set diversity
strengths: Advanced age (82) realistic for degenerative conduction disease
Progressive near-syncopal episodes showing disease trajectory
